overview:
**Very High fire danger today, driven by critically dry fuels — ERC at 91% (exceeding the local threshold) and minimum humidity down to 23%.**
- 2 of 7 local watchout thresholds are met today (ERC and minimum RH); danger eases to High tomorrow through the week as ERC and BI trend down into the 60s-80s.
- Afternoon/evening thunderstorm chances (30%) with light NE winds around 11 mph should bring scattered showers today and most of the week, per NWS.
- No active Red Flag Warning is posted for the area as of this morning.
- Stage 2 fire restrictions remain in force on BLM Uncompahgre/Gunnison/Tres Rios lands and GMUG National Forest, covering Montrose, Ouray, San Miguel, Delta, Gunnison and Dolores counties — no campfires or charcoal grills.
- The Gold Mountain Fire near Ouray has burned about 37,809 acres and is 13% contained; the Elk Fire north of Lake City was estimated at 2,274 acres Saturday with no containment estimate available.
lead: Very High fire danger today, driven by critically dry fuels — ERC at 91% (exceeding the local threshold) and minimum humidity down to 23%.

Aspen Acres fire surpasses 100,000 acres as crews increase containment to more than 60%
